name: Azitra, Inc.
business: Azitra, Inc. is an early-stage clinical biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative therapies for precision dermatology using engineered proteins and topical live biotherapeutic products. The company has built a proprietary platform that includes a microbial library of approximately 1,500 unique bacterial strains for screening therapeutic characteristics. Initial lead programs target Netherton syndrome (ATR-12), EGFRi-associated rash (ATR-04), and ichthyosis vulgaris (ATR-01), with no commercial operations to date.
sellsTo: Cancer patients undergoing EGFRi targeted therapy
Potential out-licensing partners in biopharmaceutical industry

exposures.sentence: We are an early-stage clinical biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative therapies for precision dermatology using engineered proteins and topical live biotherapeutic products.
The platform is augmented by an artificial intelligence and machine learning technology that analyzes, predicts and helps screen our library of strains for druglike molecules.

catalysts.event: Dosed first patient in ATR-12 Phase 1b clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: We dosed the first patient in August 2024.

catalysts.event: Reported initial safety results from ATR-12 Phase 1b trial
catalysts.sentence: We reported initial safety results of the first five patients dosed in the trial in June 2025.

catalysts.event: Obtained Fast Track designation for ATR-04 from FDA
catalysts.sentence: In September 2024, we obtained Fast Track designation by the FDA in this indication.

catalysts.event: Dosed first patient in ATR-04 Phase 1/2 clinical trial
catalysts.sentence: We dosed the first patient in the Phase 1/2 clinical trial in the third quarter of 2025.

catalysts.event: Completed lead optimization studies for ATR-01
catalysts.sentence: We completed lead optimization studies in 2025 and continue to perform IND-enabling studies in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: ATR-12 represents a potential $250 million global sales opportunity by mid-2030.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on the Barbati and Sun Studies, we believe that ATR-12 represents a potential $250 million global sales opportunity by mid-2030.

tamClaims.claim: ATR-04 represents a potential $1 billion global sales opportunity by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe this product candidate represents a potential $1 billion global sales opportunity by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Ichthyosis vulgaris has an estimated total patient population of 1.3 million in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: A chronic, xerotic (abnormally dry), scaly skin disease with an estimated incidence and prevalence of 1 in 250, which suggests a total patient population of 1.3 million in the United States.
